applicability: Applies to circular 0.5 mm, 2 mm, and 5 mm GaN/III-N membranes in an approximately 15 mm by 15 mm silicon-framed sample, with approximately 38 to 57 um of CVD diamond grown at approximately 720 to 750 degrees Celsius.
method_summary: The paper combines analytical modelling, COMSOL thermal-stress FEM, surface profilometry, Raman spectroscopy, and cross-sectional HAADF-STEM/EDX.
public_summary: Published literature reports that membrane bow increased strongly with diameter and that the 5 mm membrane suffered thermally coupled damage; this is source-documented evidence, not an automatic architecture recommendation.
review_notes: Draft literature transcription pending final human review.

source.reference: arXiv:2102.09664; Cuenca et al.; Thermal stress modelling of diamond on GaN/III-Nitride membranes; 2021

title: Experimental and modelling evidence for thermal stress and bow in diamond-on-GaN membranes
uncertainty_summary: Reported values are literature-transcribed values; raw measurement files are not in this repository; the 5 mm bow is an estimated maximum net bow; the paper reports the largest profilometry standard deviation as 2 um.
